title: Black timer text and End Turn button

requirements.text: In `frontend/src/components/GameTimer.tsx`, update the timer text styling so that both the match countdown and the conquest countdown labels and time values are rendered in black color regardless of warning/critical state transitions. Ensure the pulsing warning (orange) and critical (red) states only affect background or border indicators, not the text color itself.

quotes: deixe o tempo esrito em preto

acceptanceCriteria: Match countdown text is black at all times during normal gameplay.
Conquest countdown text is black at all times, including when the conquest countdown is active.
Warning and critical visual states (orange/red) do not override the text color to a non-black value.
Timer remains legible against the parchment background.

requirements.text: Add an 'End Turn' button to the game HUD in `frontend/src/pages/GamePage.tsx`. The button must be prominently visible during gameplay, styled consistently with the 16th-century parchment theme. When a human-controlled player clicks 'End Turn', the current player's turn is concluded and the game engine advances to the next player's turn (calling the appropriate turn-advance logic in `frontend/src/hooks/useGameEngine.ts`). The button should be disabled or hidden when it is not the local human player's turn (e.g., during bot turns or animations).

quotes: tenha um botao em que os jogadores apertem para finalizar o turno

acceptanceCriteria: An 'End Turn' button is visible on the game screen during a human player's turn.
Clicking 'End Turn' advances the turn to the next player.
The button is disabled or hidden when it is a bot's turn or during movement/action animations.
The button is styled with the existing parchment/period theme (earthy colors, period typography).
Bot turns continue to execute automatically without requiring the button.

constraints: Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
All changes must remain consistent with the existing 16th-century parchment visual theme.
nonGoals: Changing any other timer styling properties beyond text color.
Altering bot AI turn logic beyond wiring the End Turn button.
Adding new gameplay mechanics or balance changes.
